title: CLAUDE.md — ackwards
text: ackwards R package implementing Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern descendants (PCA / EFA / ESEM engines) hierarchical structural analysis. Extract solutions 1..k factors, characterize hierarchy via -level factor-score correlations. Full rationale, contracts, object spec, resolved defaults DESIGN.md. Note: Forbes (2023) footnote 3 cites package (github.com/jmgirard/ackwards) reference implementation extended bass-ackwards approach. Fidelity paper’s algorithm baseline contract anything Forbes-related; additive enrichments acceptable default output must reproduce Forbes’s examples exactly. contract test-backed (M44): tests/testthat/test-forbes-fidelity.R reproduces paper’s three simulation studies expected values computed Forbes’s reference implementation (OSF pcwm8; provenance fixture), M44 feasibility study additionally verified 155-variable AMH applied example 3.9e-14 (committed test — AMH matrix carries OSF license; see ROADMAP.md unscheduled items).

title: CLAUDE.md — ackwards
text: encode hard-won reasoning design phase. Changing design decision, refactor. One edge path. -level correlations go compute_edges(). Use algebra (W'RW, standardized) scoring linear; materialize scores nonlinear (EAP) user asks. Always standardize real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)) — never assume unit variance (Bartlett/oblique scores unit-variance). Keep cross-check. Retain scores route even algebra default, keep test asserting agree within tolerance linear engines. Light core, heavy opt-. object always carries loadings/variance/fit/weights/edges/ lineage/R/meta. scores, raw fits, raw data NULL default recomputable. Sign alignment anchors primary parent, “positive” (’s impossible). Lineage lives edges, never IDs. m{k}f{j} stable labels; parentage edge structure. Loud defaults. Announce consequential auto-choices via cli (e.g., ordinal-detection warning). Advise loudly; never switch basis silently. Convergence data, error. non-converging level warns + skipped; object still builds deepest converged level. Never let one bad level abort run.

title: CLAUDE.md — ackwards
text: R >= 4.1 (native pipe |> \(x) lambdas allowed). Standard devtools loop: Efficiency (don’t re-run suite needlessly). suite parallel since M48 (Config/testthat/parallel, slowest files first): ~27s TESTTHAT_CPUS=8, ~81s serial — always prefix suite runs TESTTHAT_CPUS=8 (testthat defaults 2 workers without ). check() still runs full suite examples, covr::package_coverage() runs suite — test() → check() → coverage() one gate executes suite ~3×. Vignette rebuild now cheap: seven eight vignettes precomputed (CRAN 2026-07-05 flagged old ~317s vignette rebuild) — authored vignettes/*.Rmd.orig, knitted ahead time static *.Rmd (results + vignettes/assets/ figures baked ) vignettes/precompute.R; ackwards-interpret.Rmd stays live (needs full bfi25 IPIP-label lesson). editing *.Rmd.orig, re-run Rscript vignettes/precompute.R commit regenerated *.Rmd + vignettes/assets/ — .orig script .Rbuildignored, un-regenerated edit ships stale output. Iterate targeted devtools::test(filter = "<x>") / testthat::test_file(); run failing tests way shows details (capture res <- use non-silent reporter — never silent--rerun); skip vignette rebuild mid-work checks check(vignettes = FALSE); final gate run Rscript tools/dod-gate.R — executes whole DoD sequence (check → coverage → style → lint → pkgdown) serially one process sensible TESTTHAT_CPUS, prints/exits failure. Never run two package-touching R processes concurrently. Two transcript-mined anti-patterns avoid (M48): bare devtools::load_all() Rscript call nothing persistent (Rscript fresh process; test()/check() load package ), cd <repo> && … compound commands trigger avoidable permission prompts — use absolute paths. tests, reuse cached() fit memo (tests/testthat/helper-data.R) instead refitting identical ackwards() objects — never reproducibility/serial-vs-parallel oracles (cached second call asserts nothing) fits wrapped condition expectations; treat cached objects read-— rebinding returned value copy--modify safe, environment-bearing components (e.g. lavaan fits keep_fits = TRUE) shared reference across cache hits. Scaffolding helpers: usethis::use_r(), use_test(), use_package(), use_testthat(3), use_github_action("check-standard"). Use testthat 3e, roxygen2 exported functions (document default, runnable @examples, @seealso cross-links).
code: devtools::load_all()      # load for interactive testing devtools::document()      # regenerate roxygen docs + NAMESPACE after any roxygen change devtools::test()          # run testthat suite devtools::check()         # full R CMD check styler::style_pkg()       # format lintr::lint_package()     # lint

title: CLAUDE.md — ackwards
text: Don’t commit milestone work (anything touching R/, tests/, DESCRIPTION, vignettes) straight master. Trivial isolated doc fixes may go direct user’s discretion — push immediately. unpushed commit master leaves local ahead origin/master; git pull won’t surface (nothing fetch), next milestone branch — cut local master — bundle milestone’s squash-merge force post-merge divergence. /plan-milestone step 8a now guards branching ahead--origin master. Exception — release / CRAN-submission milestones (e.g. CRAN-prep, version-bump/release): wait full green CI matrix merging, CRAN runs exactly matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu × release/devel/oldrel) reject platform failures local macOS check() can’t see. , don’t merge local-green alone. exception also reactivates package real users collaborators (red master blocks others / ships bugs) — treat transition trigger reconsider branch protection. touch legacy branch v0-legacy tag — preserve pre-AI code. Small, focused commits imperative messages (e.g., Add PCA engine level contract). Don’t force-push master. Don’t commit data, credentials, large binaries.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Implement Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern descendants: extract series factor/component solutions 1 k, characterize hierarchy correlations factor/component scores adjacent (, extensions, ) levels. package provides multiple extraction engines, principled tooling choosing k, sign/lineage-aware naming, tidy outputs, attractive console + graphical reporting.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Prior art. psych::bassAckward() (Revelle) already implements core loop PCA EFA rotation tenBerge scores. reimplement factor extraction numerics; wrap established engines add psych . contribution. - first-class ESEM engine (lavaan), bringing Mplus psychopathology/HiTOP workflow R — per-level fit indices SEs, ordinal/Likert support — psych::bassAckward provide. (Kim & Eaton 2015; Forbush et al. 2024 reference workflows.) - Proper ordinal/Likert handling (polychoric basis, ordinal estimators). - Forbes (2023) extended method (redundancy + artifact pruning via standalone prune() verb, -levels correlations). - replicability gate hierarchy depth: split-half factor comparability (Everett 1983; Goldberg 1990 — method inventor’s quality gate, dropped modern ESEM/HiTOP lineage) standalone comparability() verb (M46; §14.35), recommended end--end workflow documented vignette("ackwards-girard"). - clean, tidy, serializable result object print/summary/tidy/glance/autoplot. - lineage-aligned layered diagram rather misleading tree. Honesty caveat state docs print. bass-ackwards result series linked solutions, fitted hierarchical model. “hierarchy” descriptive/emergent (edges score correlations), higher-order SEM. psych’s docs make point; .

Rotation single fixed choice across engines: varimax (orthogonal; T'=T^-1 property enables closed-form W’RW algebra). Oblique rotation scope — see §9 §14.1. Engine interface (contract). Every engine, level k = 1..K, returns standardized per-level object: level fails converge recorded, warned , skipped, never thrown. hierarchy built deepest converged level.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized — R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} Λ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

what: 5.2 Scoring descriptor (returned per level by each engine)
title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
code: scoring <- list(   linear    = TRUE,                # are scores a fixed linear map S = Z W?   method    = "tenBerge",          # "components" | "regression" | "bartlett" | "tenBerge" | "EAP" | ...   basis     = "pearson",           # "pearson" | "polychoric" | "spearman"   weights   = W,                   # p x k score-coefficient matrix; NULL if !linear   score_var = v                    # length-k vector = diag(W'RW); NULL if !linear )

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring — EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear → algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one “edge” represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see §7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: S3, R6/S4 — matches psych/lavaan/broom conventions serializes cleanly. Storage rule. Light tidy core always; heavy artifacts opt-. - Always: loadings, variance, fit/convergence, weight matrices, within-level correlations, edges, lineage, p x p input R, meta. enough diagram + interpretation small shareable. - Opt-: factor scores (O(n × Σk); also privacy/sharing liability), raw fits (lavaan fits can large), raw data. Defaults: scores = FALSE, keep_fits = FALSE, keep R = TRUE. directly answers “giant list fitted models vs. just loadings/scores/correlations” question: neither — structured light core, heavy bits nullable recomputable.
code:
structure(list( call, engine, rotation, cor, n_obs, k_max, seed, pkg_version, levels = , edges = , lineage = , scores = NULL, # opt-in (large n; privacy); accessor recomputes on demand fits = NULL, # opt-in raw engine objects (keep_fits = TRUE) r =

, # cheap; kept so scores/edges recomputable data = NULL, # opt-in raw data meta = (M36), item_means/item_sds = (M45 -- the frame of reference for out-of-sample scoring), min_eigenvalue = , near_singular = >, prune = NULL # Forbes extension: node flags + chain table + phi table; # populated by prune(x, ...) -- a standalone verb piped off # ackwards(), not an ackwards() argument (M34; see s.14) ), class = "ackwards")

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1…mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parent’s position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces can’t eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layout’s barycenter step (§11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), don’t encode parentage m{k}f{j} — breaks structure isn’t clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e. apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected — adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs k−1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. Tucker’s φ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, §14.19) used matching . Sign alignment — anchor primary parent, “positive.” Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative. Implementation note (M35): “propagating top-” means child’s flip chosen parent’s already-aligned sign, parent’s raw orientation — otherwise flipped parent leaves primary edge displaying negative. every primary-parent edge non-negative; secondary edges may red.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: suggest_k() returns several complementary criteria consensus range, never single number. five criteria implemented (M12): Selective computation (M25). criteria argument (rlang::arg_match(multiple = TRUE), default five) lets callers request subset c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"). Skipping real compute saving, output filtering: pa_pc/pa_fa share one fa.parallel(fa = "") call (neither run) map/vss share one vss() call, e.g. criteria = "map" avoids PA simulation entirely; vss toggles VSS-1+VSS-2 unit. Non-requested k_* fields NA; criteria data frame keeps stable schema (NA-filled non-run columns); object records criteria_requested. print()/autoplot() render requested criteria consensus range computed requested criteria . Empirical Kaiser Criterion (EKC) EGA (EGAnet) scope — additional deps without sufficient incremental benefit five criteria . Note: suggest_k() accepts cor = "pearson" (default) "spearman" ; "polychoric" supported (PA VSS polychoric eigen-decomposition path). Users analysing ordinal data run suggest_k() Pearson matrix switch cor = "polychoric" ackwards(). Report k maximum depth; users often deliberately set level two past consensus watch factors fragment. Note overextraction/non-replicability caution (Forbes 2023). Add seed argument reproducibility CD step — psych::fa.parallel() respond reliably set.seed(), PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. See vignette("ackwards-suggest-k") narrative/educational treatment five criteria including pros/cons, bias direction, engine--criterion pairing (M14). suggest_k() answers “depth range plausible” eigenstructure. companion question — “factors actually replicate” — answered directly standalone comparability() verb (M46, §14.35): split-half factor comparability per level per factor, depth floor suggest_k()’s ceiling. two deliberately separate functions: suggest_k() pre-fit screen eigenvalue criteria, comparability() engine-specific fit-based.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: print.ackwards — compact cli “card”: call, engine, rotation, cor basis, n, level range, per-level convergence, deepest usable level, count edges show-cut. matrix dumps. States “series linked solutions, fitted hierarchy” caveat . summary.ackwards — per-level variance explained fit indices (ESEM); readable lineage list (m1f1 → m2f1, m2f2 → …); flagged redundant/artifact components object pruned via prune(). top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort, , show_labels) (M18; /show_labels M36) — salient-item listing, filtered |loading| >= cut sorted descending. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. = "factor" (default) groups items factor; = "item" inverts list, per item, factors loads (cross-loading view); n/sort apply within chosen unit. fit data carried per-column "label" attributes (captured meta$item_labels), show_labels = TRUE (default) prints items code: label per-item bare-code fallback. Loadings reflect primary-parent sign alignment (Inv. 4). $data field subset tidy(= "loadings") table (plus label column labels available). tidy(x, = "edges") (default) — one row per directed edge (m_k f_i, m_{k+1} f_j, r, is_primary, above_cut). graph edge list primary plotting input. tidy(x, = "loadings") — long format (level, factor, item, loading), sign-aligned; feeds loading heatmaps. tidy(x, = "variance") — per-factor variance level: (level, factor, proportion, cumulative), 0-1 scale (M32; matches engine’s internal representation broom/psych convention — percent formatting display concern print/summary, tidy() concern). tidy(x, = "fit") — per-level fit statistic: (level, statistic, value) (M32: column renamed index, read like row position rather fit-statistic name — holds fit-index names EFA/ESEM eigenvalue positions PCA). format = "wide" pivots one row per level. pass/fail cutoffs=/meets output (removed M32) — conventional Hu & Bentler (1999) thresholds conventional contested, tidy() reports values ; autoplot(= "fit") summary() render reference lines/inline annotations. glance(x) — one row model-level meta (engine, k_max, n, deepest converged, cor basis). as_tibble(x) → tidy(x, "edges"). augment(x, data, append, id_cols, scaling) / predict(x, newdata, scaling) (M45) — per-observation factor scores, including --sample scoring (fit training split, score test split without retraining). scaling = "fit" (default) standardizes supplied data fit-time item moments stored meta scores share training metric; "sample" re-standardizes supplied data’s moments (pre-M45 behaviour; option cor-matrix objects, carry moments). predict() thin exported wrapper returning exactly augment(x, data = newdata, append = FALSE, scaling = scaling) — idiomatic front door (psych::predict.psych / lavPredict precedent).

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Split layout (light, core) rendering (optional, Suggests). keeps dependency footprint sane lets users plot layout however like. Top-pass – determines left--right order level. factor’s rank |r|-weighted mean parents’ ranks, grouping siblings share parent minimising crossings. Bottom-pass – assigns actual x coordinates. deepest level (level k) evenly spaced; every upper-level factor placed simple mean x primary children: factor one primary child lands directly ; factor multiple primary children lands exactly halfway . Falls back |r|-weighted mean children factors primary children. Spreading enforces min_sep adjacent nodes needed. level-1 node shifted x = 0 passes. produces “boxes aligned primary-child structure” look owner wants. autoplot.ackwards() / plot() (Suggests: ggplot2). Renders layered diagram ba_layout() + edge tibble. Edge encodings user-assignable always legended (M35): sign_by chooses channel sign (color default; also linetype, , none) magnitude_by chooses channel |r| (linewidth default, none). aesthetic mapped without matching legend. (pre-M35 strong/weak cut_strong linetype split retired — double-encoded magnitude legend-less.) Nodes labeled m{k}f{j}, optionally substantive labels top-loading items. direction toggles vertical (default, level 1 top) vs. horizontal (level 1 left) orientation. Forbes extension rendering (complex — phase ). Keep layout; annotate rather re-layout: fade/strike pruned (redundant/artifact) nodes, allow skip-level edges (longer curves) since extension correlates levels, just adjacent. Default showing -cut edges control clutter. Treat later milestone; ship clean adjacent-level Goldberg diagram first. fully-pruned level (every factor flagged) left/bottom axis label rendered italic (M40) normal render path, denoting status alongside grey node fill; partially-pruned levels keep plain label, drop_pruned = TRUE fully-pruned level’s nodes removed label drawn. label_template(x, style) (M18, core, heavy deps). Generates named-character-vector scaffold autoplot(node_labels=). Styles: "id" (round-trip identity), "forbes" (level- letter + within-level index), "blank" (empty strings). Returns vector also prints editable c(...) literal copy-paste. Factor IDs canonical ba_layout() order.

EFAtools (M12) future.apply (M26) gated behind rlang::is_installed() — never hard-required. Gate every Suggests use rlang::check_installed() helpful cli message naming engine needs . Rcpp dependency planned (see §3).

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation → varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 — confounds cross-level signal method’s whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis → pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order → primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring → tenBerge polychoric (linear → algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name → ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1–M3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: ≤ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: κ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine — engines hardcoded cfT → "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(κ=1/p) ≡ varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine → lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring → self-compute tenBerge weights lavaan’s estimated loadings Λ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, Λ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring → scope (declined M28). EAP’s shrinkage attenuates cross-level correlations — primary signal bass-ackwards measures — making theoretically inferior tenBerge method. EAP request still returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved, implementing EAP planned. 14. cor = "polychoric" → general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaan’s latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator → WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se → added §4 level contract (p×k matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11–16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error → warn + truncate → object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API → two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). Superseded M34 (item 27 ): pruning now standalone prune() verb, ackwards() argument, pairs longer auto-upgrades. 18. Prune action → flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation — say print/docs (extends §2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy → faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| ≥ .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). Tucker’s φ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. φ formula Σaᵢbᵢ / sqrt(Σaᵢ² · Σbᵢ²); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches Forbes’s examples): always report r φ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second — borderline cases like paper’s .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity — chain answers “perpetuates every level,” endpoint r answers “construct”). 21. Artifact → never auto-flagged. prune(x, "artifact") surfaces φ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf. Wicherts et al. 2016). Resolved M32 (API-shape & naming; owner-reviewed, equivalent guidance elsewhere document): 22. tidy(x, = "fit") long-format key column → statistic, replacing index (read row position; actually held fit-index names EFA/ESEM eigenvalue positions PCA). Wide format (format = "wide") unaffected — column names still come values key. 23. k_max naming collision (ackwards() = extraction depth vs. suggest_k() = max factors/components evaluated) → keep shared name functions. ’re genuinely dial applied different workflow stages (suggest_k(k_max = ...) → ackwards(k_max = ...)); renaming one create vocabulary drift without removing real ambiguity. Resolved instead via roxygen: function’s @param k_max states meaning cross-references ’s. 24. tidy(= "fit") cutoff flags → removed. cutoffs = TRUE argument meets/{statistic}_meets columns produced (.flag_fit()) dropped; pass/fail boolean quietly endorsed Hu & Bentler (1999) thresholds package elsewhere (§9, section) treats conventional contested — continuing M28/M31 output-honesty trajectory. .fit_cutoffs() retained internal helper: autoplot(= "fit") still draws threshold reference lines summary() still annotates inline check/cross mark: visual guides, returned pass/fail column user mistake computed judgment. 25. Variance scale → proportion, 0-1 (tidy(= "variance") columns renamed variance_pct/cumulative_pct → proportion/cumulative, values divided 100). aligns tidy() engine’s internal variance slot (already 0-1; see print.ackwards, reads directly) broom/psych convention (psych reports “Proportion Var” 0-1). Percent formatting moves display layer (print(), summary(), vignette gt tables) rather living tidy data. 26. M31-deferred: effective ESEM estimator recorded $meta. M31 explicitly deferred (“better bundled M32’s meta/column decisions bolted ”): x$meta$estimator now stores effective estimator auto-selection ("ML"/"MLR"/"WLSMV"/"ULSMV"; NA PCA/EFA). summary() gains one-line footnote naming lavaan’s scaled-variant reporting (§14.M31 point 2/Post-review) whenever effective estimator "WLSMV"/"ULSMV"/"MLR" — shown "ML", scaled variant. five M32 changes breaking deprecation path (pre-CRAN, users; consistent M34 pruning-verb precedent clean moves compatibility shims). Resolved M34 (pruning verb; owner-reviewed, breaking, deprecation path — pre-CRAN, users): 27. Pruning extracted standalone, pipeable S3 generic prune() (prune.ackwards), ackwards() argument. five prune-related args (prune → renamed rules, redundancy_r, redundancy_phi, min_items, orphan_r) leave ackwards() entirely: ackwards(...) |> prune(...). Rationale: extraction expensive, deterministic step; pruning cheap interpretive. Separating lets researcher re-prune new thresholds without re-extracting (re-running ESEM per level expensive path M26 optimized). prune() generic (UseMethod), plain function, coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g. rpart::prune) regardless package load order. Returns ackwards object $prune populated (replacing prior pruning) — new class, print/summary/tidy/glance/augment/ autoplot work unchanged. 28. Edges redundancy chains artifact φ recomputed fresh inside prune() via compute_edges(pairs = "") object’s stored levels/r (Invariant 3), never written back x$edges (Invariant 1: one edge path). ackwards()‘s pairs auto-upgrade "" pruning requested (M5 item 17) removed along — pairs now pure display/storage setting ackwards(), decoupled pruning; prune() works correctly regardless fit-time pairs value. 29. Manual pruning: prune(x, rules = "none", manual = c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flags user-named nodes directly (standalone, auto rule needed), unions onto auto rule’s flags (prune(x, "redundant", manual = c(...))). Unknown labels error. overlap auto rule manual, auto rule’s prune_reason wins (informative); otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". 30. Naming: canonical rule name "artifact" (US spelling, owner’s preference), "artefact" accepted alias (nod Commonwealth spelling Forbes’ usage) normalized internally "artifact". Existing code passing "artefact" keeps working. "tucker" considered rejected alias/rename: mode surfaces Tucker’s φ (also computes few_items/orphan/split_merge structural signals), naming statistic mislabel umbrella — "artifact" names mode . 31. ackwards() explicitly rejects five removed args passed via ... (rather silently absorbing ) pointer prune() — silent absorption masked-argument footgun, clean break intended (Invariant 6: loud, silent). Resolved M38 (missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA; reverses resolved default — owner-signed-): 32. FIML promoted first-class PCA/EFA route. M16 contract “missing = "fiml" errors PCA, EFA” reversed Pearson basis: engine = "pca"/"efa" cor = "pearson", missing = "fiml" now routes R psych::corFiml() (full-information ML, MVN) feeds existing W'RW algebra — Invariant-1-clean (one edge path, one corFiml call per run since R computed reused across levels; new dependency, psych already Imports). still errors non-Pearson PCA/EFA basis (Spearman, polychoric — corFiml honor ) WLSMV/ULSMV. .resolve_missing() gained cor argument enforce guard matrix. Grew M37 doc-planning observation user already smuggle FIML via M22 correlation-matrix seam (ackwards(corFiml(x), …)); M38 makes capability discoverable owns n_obs tradeoff. 33. n_obs string option + default. raw-data FIML PCA/EFA path, n_obs accepts "total" (default) "complete". "total" = every row contributing FIML likelihood, matching convention genuine FIML analysis reports (Enders 2010) giving cross-engine parallelism ESEM-ML FIML. Point estimates (loadings/edges) depend choice; EFA fit indices , approximate two-step (FIML matrix → normal-theory EFA) procedure regardless N (Zhang & Savalei 2020) — route announces caveat via cli (Invariant 6). "effective" considered dropped: canonical formula exists corFiml→EFA route, package-invented convention masquerading standard. string n_obs rejected path; cor-matrix input still requires numeric N. Resolved M45 (--sample scoring; owner-approved 2026-07-01): 34. Scoring standardizes fit-time moments default. augment()’s scaling argument defaults "fit": supplied data standardized training means/SDs (stored meta$item_means/item_sds since M45) weight matrices apply. Rationale: “applying trained model” means — test observation’s score must depend observations share split, train/test scores must share one metric (cross-validation use case motivated milestone). "sample" (pre-M45 behaviour) remains explicit opt-deliberately re-standardizing sample different population metric, option cor-matrix objects. Breaking subset/new-data scoring values (pre-CRAN, deprecation path; M32/M34 precedent). companion predict.ackwards() export chosen augment-surface discoverability replicators (psych/lavaan users reach predict()). Resolved M46 (Girard extension — replicability gate; owner-approved 2026-07-01): 35. Split-half factor comparability standalone verb. comparability(data, k_max, engine,     cor, fm, n_splits = 10, seed) resurrects Everett (1983) / Goldberg (1990) factor comparability coefficients hierarchy-depth gate, extended every bass-ackwards level. Mechanics: full-sample ackwards() fit anchors labels (results report m{k}f{j} user see); per split, levels 1..k fit independently random half via engine internals; half-solution matched anchor greedy--removal max-|r| bijection (square, -k — well-posed , unlike §7 parent matching; clue dependency, near-ties instability measured); coefficient correlation two matched half-solution scores pooled correlation matrix, computed compute_edges() two-element levels list (Invariant 1 — one edge path), signed orienting matched factors positively toward anchor (negative coefficient stays visible diagnostic). Tucker’s φ matched loading columns reported alongside (score agreement vs. pattern agreement). Report-first, judge-never: nothing auto-flagged; conventional .90/.95 benchmarks appear reference lines / footer prose (M32 cutoff philosophy). Scope: PCA/EFA (ESEM deferred — 2 * n_splits lavaan hierarchies need performance treatment; logged ROADMAP.md) pearson/spearman (suggest_k() precedent: polychoric estimation every half-sample slow NPD-prone; screen Pearson, fit final model polychoric). Repeated splits default n_splits = 10 (Goldberg’s practice; single split misleads luck draw). Per-half engine warnings muffled convergence shortfall summarised (summary$n_splits_ok). recommended workflow — suggest_k ceiling → comparability floor → fit → prune → interpret → --sample validation — documented vignette("ackwards-girard") named replicability-gated (Girard) workflow. Bootstrap edge CIs standalone verb. boot_edges(x, data, n_boot = 1000, conf = 0.95, seed) attaches nonparametric bootstrap SEs + percentile CIs every edge — inferential-honesty companion point-estimate prune() rules Forbes strongest- edge practice (resurrects §14 e4 deferral). standalone verb (ackwards() argument), consistent M34 direction extracting expensive/optional work pipeable verbs (prune(), comparability()); raw data re-supplied light core store (Invariant 3). Mechanics per replicate: resample rows replacement → recompute R fit’s cor/missing routine → refit levels 1..k_max (engine internals, muffled) → anchor replicate level full-sample solution (item-35 greedy-max-|r| matching + sign orientation, evaluated full-sample R) → edges via compute_edges() replicate R (Invariant 1). Anchoring load-bearing: without , factor label-switching sign-flipping across replicates corrupt pooled distributions. resample indices drawn upfront seed, replicate deterministic given indices serial future.apply-parallel paths (M26 pattern) agree bit--bit — design choice makes “serial ≡ parallel” test-assertable. Failed replicates drop NA counted per edge (n_boot_ok), never aborting (Invariant 7). Output attaches x$boot; tidy(= "edges") gains se/lo/hi/n_boot_ok, print/summary note coverage. Scope: PCA/EFA + pearson/spearman , mirroring item 35 (ESEM = n_boot lavaan hierarchies; polychoric = per-resample polychoric estimation, slow + NPD-prone; logged ROADMAP.md). Cor-matrix input, ESEM, polychoric objects error pointers. DESIGN §14’s original “reuse loadings_se infrastructure” phrasing amended: loadings_se lavaan’s analytic delta-method SE, analogue edges varimax-rotated hierarchies — bootstrap SE method; reused storage/tidy display pattern. Percentile (Fisher-z normal-approx) CIs: percentile respects [-1, 1] bound skew r near 0.9 prune threshold. Oracle note: full-pipeline bootstrap SE legitimately exceeds Fisher-z analytic SE materialized-score correlation, replicate re-extracts factors (loading uncertainty) Fisher-z treats scores fixed observed variables; exact Fisher-z match holds fixed-weights percentile bootstrap (halves test-asserted). intervals per-edge error bars, familywise correction — make edge’s precision visible undo selection bias scanning many edges strongest (stated object docs + girard vignette). Resolved M50 (release polish; owner-approved 2026-07-02): 37. bfi25 carries public-domain IPIP variable labels. item column gets IPIP marker stem (Goldberg, 1999; ipip.ori.org) plain label attribute, populating M36 capture path top_items() prints code: label zero setup. Sourced directly public-domain IPIP items (text necessarily identical psych::bfi.dictionary — public pool — framed copy). Plain attributes dropped base row-subsetting (na.omit(), [), unlike class-based labelled/haven vectors M36 targets, documented pattern fit dataset directly (missing = "listwise") rather pre-filtering. label_items() setter third teaching dataset declined (logged M49 Phase avoid double-logging); persistent factor-label pipeline (distinct item labels) deferred 0.2.0. 38. suggest_k() ordinal-detection warning (Invariant 6 symmetry). suggest_k() now runs detect_ordinal() raw-data input warns per session, matching ackwards() comparability(). suggest_k() screens Pearson/Spearman basis design, wording points final ackwards() fit (cor = "polychoric"), suggest_k() — () switch basis . Guarded raw-data path (correlation matrix items inspect). 39. Console/example polish (contract change). Engine name renders lowercase across print methods; summary() per-level figures use fixed decimals trailing zeros; pruned footers consolidate one note. Roxygen examples split intent: mechanics demos use continuous sim16 (ordinal warning, faster checks), content demos keep bfi25 polychoric basis. display/documentation choices, behavioural defaults. Resolved M49 Phase (roadmap cleanup; owner-approved 2026-07-02): 40. Dual EFA chi-squares (review enhancement e2) — declined. proposed chi_empirical column (psych’s residual-based $chi) reported alongside likelihood-ratio chi EFA fit row now carries (M42/C1 fix). Rejected net-negative: re-opens exact mispairing C1 fixed (second chi-square inviting users pair wrong p-value); NA-heavy, EFA-column fit schema shared ESEM (empirical chi-square), adding asymmetry + doc burden; zero downstream consumer package (anyone wants can read keep_fits = TRUE object). shape M40 categorical decline: confusion surface capability. Removed ROADMAP.md. 41. Item-label ecosystem — one shipped, two declined, one deferred. M50 shipped bfi25’s public-domain IPIP variable labels (item 37). Three adjacent asks resolved avoid double-logging: () label_items() setter declined — duplicate labelled::var_label() (established idiom .capture_item_labels() already reads) , worse, deepen label overload item labels label_template()’s factor labels; attr(col, "label") <- ... / labelled::var_label() route documented instead. (b) third teaching dataset declined — sim16 (idealized continuous) bfi25 (realistic ordinal) deliberate two-foil pair; third dilutes contrast without adding distinct teaching case. (c) persistent factor-label pipeline (set_factor_labels()-style setter honored autoplot/print/summary/tidy, label_template() scaffold) deferred 0.2.0 (declined): purely additive, 0.1.0 loses nothing, deserves naming/storage/precedence design. item-vs-factor “label” vocabulary split load-bearing — two concepts must stay lexically distinct docs future API. (Banked ROADMAP.md.) Resolved M49 (polychoric robustness fix; owner-approved 2026-07-02): 42. correct argument exposes polychoric continuity correction. Real-world ordinal data surfaced hard failure: psych::polychoric() errors default continuity correction (correct = 0.5) item near-empty (singleton) response category items unequal category counts produce sparse cross-cell, — psych runs item pairs parallel — one failure collapses whole call opaque supply 'x' 'y' message. psych’s advice correct = 0, ackwards() gave way pass . Resolution: ackwards() gains correct = 0.5 (matching psych’s default name discoverability), forwarded psych::polychoric() PCA/EFA polychoric path (ESEM computes polychoric inside lavaan; Pearson/Spearman ignore ). Additive, non-breaking — default reproduces prior behaviour. failure message rewritten name correct = 0 remedy instead passing psych’s opaque error , NPD guard hardened catch polychoric matrix NA/NaN entries (naming offending items) eigen(), rather crashing base R’s “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed”. reimplementation polychoric — wrap--diagnose fix (§ “wrap established engines”). 43. check_items() — pre-analysis item screen (new export). correct fix (item 42) reactive; stop near-constant item silently producing plausible-looking meaningless factor, name offender, catch truly constant item (psych::polychoric() silently deletes, crashing downstream subscript bounds). check_items(data, cor) reports, one row per item, stats predict failures (n_valid, pct_missing, n_distinct, min_count, top_prop) worst-case flag (constant / near-constant / sparse category / high missing / ok). Thresholds deliberately conservative — constant = one distinct value; near-constant = category holding ≥ 95% responses (case actually breaks polychoric); sparse category = smallest observed category < 5, flagged cor = "polychoric" (rare--present Likert categories usually fit fine, report-). ackwards() runs internal screen (.screen_items(), shared — DRY): errors constant item (naming , psych can delete ) warns near-constant item, deliberately warn merely sparse category (avoids nagging ordinary ordinal data). Report-first, like suggest_k()/comparability(); never modifies data. New export, new dependency. 44. Trust guidance + durable near-singularity signal. Fit-time warnings transient (scroll ; reloaded/shared object shows none), user can trust rank-deficient fit whose CFI silently NA. Two additions: () “trust result” @section ?ackwards tiering every diagnostic ackwards raises fatal (constant item, polychoric failure, non-convergence, near-singular matrix) / caution (Heywood, near-constant item, ordinal--Pearson) / informational (pairwise-missing, sparse category, ordinal-detection Pearson intended); (b) near-singularity check (.near_singular_check(), shared raw-data cor-matrix paths — basis-agnostic: also catches redundant items Pearson/Spearman/FIML bases user-supplied matrices, just polychoric) records meta$min_eigenvalue meta$near_singular (smallest eigenvalue < 1e-4) print()/summary() re-surface durable “near-singular – fit indices scores may unreliable” caution object , just fit time. messaging avoids singling CFI (ESEM-index — EFA tell TLI/RMSEA psych’s residual fallback). Report-; changes estimate. (M49 review follow-generalized initial polychoric-implementation.) Resolved M51 (factor-label pipeline; first milestone 0.2.0 cycle; owner-approved 2026-07-02): 45. Persistent factor labels — new set_factor_labels() verb + factor_labels() getter. Deferred--declined §14.41(c); resolves naming/storage/precedence design. factor-label counterpart M50’s item labels (meta$item_labels, item 37) two must stay lexically distinct — every symbol says factor label; nothing touches item_labels. - Storage. x$meta$factor_labels: named character vector, names = factor IDs (m{k}f{j}), values = user strings. Partial labeling allowed (factor entry falls back ID everywhere). Living meta (top-level slot) means rides prune(), boot_edges(), augment(), predict() unmodified per-verb code — verbs already copy meta. NULL/absent label set (default), unlabeled object byte-identical behaviour pre-M51 one. - API — verb + getter, replacement function. set_factor_labels(x, labels) returns modified ackwards object (pipeable, matching package’s verb grammar: prune(), boot_edges()). Repeated calls merge/update (can label incrementally); labels value NULL clears labels; NA "" value given ID removes just one. setter errors (warns) name matching factor ID — typo’d ID mistake, object’s IDs knowable front (consistent package’s loud-validation posture, Invariant 6). warn two levels share value (m2f1 m3f1 “Internalizing” ordinary hierarchical case). companion getter factor_labels(x) returns stored named vector (NULL). Chose verb+getter pair factor_labels(x) <- value replacement method keep one pipeline idiom across package avoid names<--style -place mutation reads oddly next object’s otherwise-functional API. - Display form label (id). Every text surface lists factor shows Neuroticism (m5f1) label set, bare m5f1 otherwise. Keeping ID visible load-bearing: handle user needs index tidy()/edges, mirrors autoplot() keeps IDs discoverable. rule simply: wherever text surface names factor, shows label (id). Concretely: summary() (per-level variance listing lineage tree), print() (one status line noting many factors labeled, ), top_items() (factor dimension either way — group headers = "factor", body entries = "item"). autoplot() exception — diagram node shows substantive label (parenthetical ID), stored label exactly persistent default existing node_labels argument, always replaced node text wholesale. Precedence: stored labels form node-text baseline; call-time node_labels entry overrides per node (call-time beats stored; every existing script unaffected). - tidy() — label columns appear set (Invariant 5). ID columns (factor loadings/variance/scores; /edges) never mutated — lineage lives IDs. labels set, tidy() gains factor_label column (loadings/variance/scores) from_label/to_label (edges), carrying label labeled factors NA otherwise. unlabeled object’s tidy() output keeps pre-M51 column set byte--byte — zero churn existing consumers, columns conditional rather always-present. - scope verb. comparability() fits fresh hierarchies data (labeled object), takes labels. augment()/predict() score column names stay m{k}f{j} — syntactic identifiers user binds , display text. label_template() unchanged; printed c(...) literal now framed docs scaffold fill feed set_factor_labels(). 46. Factorability & data-adequacy diagnostics — new factorability() export + internal ackwards() screen (M52). Adds pre-fit “matrix worth factoring, N large enough” layer item 43 (check_items(), per-item) suggest_k() (depth) cover. new dependency (psych already supplies KMO()/cortest.bartlett(); Ledermann bound base arithmetic), signature default change ackwards(). - reports. KMO sampling adequacy (overall + per-item MSA), Bartlett’s sphericity test, N / p / N:p, Ledermann bound (largest number common factors identifiable p variables, .ledermann_bound(p) = max k ((p-k)^2 - (p+k))/2 >= 0). Computed chosen cor basis, raw data correlation matrix (N-based rows NA matrix arrives without n_obs). - Honesty stance (extends item 24). Item 24 removed Hu–Bentler fit pass/fail flags package refuses endorse contested cutoffs returned booleans. principle governs : factorability() reports values conventional bands (Kaiser KMO labels, 5:1/10:1 N:p rules, Bartlett .05), explicitly framed printout roxygen rule thumb, settled threshold (required N depends communalities overdetermination — MacCallum et al. 1999). pass/fail column returned. - Internal ackwards() screen — engine-split, advisory-(Invariant 6/7). ackwards() runs .factorability_screen() every fit emits two .frequency = "" warnings, never aborting never altering fit: () Ledermann -identification warning naming first -bound level, fired EFA/ESEM (k_max compared bound) — PCA exempt components exact linear combinations latent-model df constraint (existing k_max <= p ceiling suffices); (b) single consolidated sampling-adequacy warning KMO < 0.5, N:p < 5, N < 100, pointing factorability() full report. Ledermann warning compares requested k_max (design error flag) warns--builds — truncated level still warns separately, two complementary. Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (§9, §14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ), missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA path (M38): algebra uses psych::corFiml() matrix scores route standardizes raw, NA-bearing data (pairwise Pearson SDs), two bases diverge missingness design — reason polychoric excluded. oracle tests therefore run complete-data linear engines; FIML/polychoric object fed , false-failure risk, cross-check certify paths. - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions — resolved M10: engine now inspects lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "theta") warns (diag(theta) <= 0) (parity EFA engine, Invariant 7). - ESEM ML/MLR missing = "pairwise": lavaan uses listwise deletion model fit edges computed separately-computed pairwise stats::cor() — minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full pairwise N). Documented $meta$missing; per-call advisory warning fires whenever NAs detected. Use missing = "listwise" "fiml" resolve. (Added M16; §9 missing row cross-references .) - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + §14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Done M25 (Wave 2). prune = "artefact" now populates x$prune$structural per-factor few_items / orphan / split_merge signals (flag/report ; auto-pruning). Args: min_items = 3L, orphan_r = 0.5. split_merge TRUE factor’s primary items came multiple different primary factors preceding level. CLI print()/summary() report flagged count. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Done M25 (Wave 3). redundancy_phi = NULL now auto-resolves: PCA → φ filter; EFA/ESEM → 0.95. NA explicit opt-. Announces via cli auto-applied (Invariant 6). See §9 defaults table. - Selection bias “strongest” edge. Done M47 (item 36). boot_edges() adds nonparametric bootstrap SEs + percentile CIs every edge (PCA/EFA + pearson/spearman; upfront seeded indices → serial ≡ parallel; full-sample anchoring per replicate; future.apply dispatch; tidy/print/summary integration; girard-vignette honesty section). intervals quantify per-edge precision design correct multiplicity scanning many edges strongest — caveat documented, silently “fixed”. - M40 spin-(code/viz deferred doc-M39; shipped M40 except declined flag): - Ordinal categorical convenience flag. Declined M40 (owner sign-, 2026-07-01). proposed categorical = TRUE/FALSE argument ackwards() flipping cor (pearson→polychoric) ESEM estimator (ML/MLR→WLSMV) together. Rejected redundant: cor = "polychoric" already auto-selects WLSMV (estimator = NULL auto-rule, §9), categorical = TRUE pure synonym cor = "polychoric" — two-settings--one shortcut. add conflict surface (categorical = TRUE + cor = "pearson"?) §9 defaults change zero new capability. Discoverability handled docs layer instead (ordinal vignette already states cor = "polychoric" alone flips estimator; ordinal-detection cli warning names option runtime). R//DESCRIPTION/§9 change. - Ordinal correlation-comparison visualization. Done M40. two raw round(x$r[1:5,1:5], 2) matrix chunks vignettes/ackwards-ordinal.Rmd replaced one dodged bar chart ten N1–N5 lower-triangle item-pair correlations, fill = basis (Pearson vs polychoric), reshape code hidden. Viz-; ggplot2 already Suggests; package-code dependency change. (gt long-format Δ-table alternative set aside — vignette already carries two gt Δ-tables, chart adds variety directly addresses “matrices hard compare”.) - Forbes pruned-level axis-label styling. Done M40. fully-pruned level (every factor flagged) now gets italic autoplot() axis label normal (non-drop_pruned) render path, denoting status alongside existing grey node fill; partially-pruned levels keep plain label. Automatic (new argument). See §11.

(1990). alternative “description personality”: Big-Five factor structure. J. Personality Social Psychology, 59(6), 1216–1229. Goldberg, L. R. (2006). bass-ackwards. J. Research Personality, 40(4), 347–358. Waller, N. (2007). general method computing hierarchical component structures… JRP, 41(4), 745–752. Kim, H., & Eaton, N. R. (2001). overview analytic rotation exploratory factor analysis. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 36(1), 111–150. Revelle, W. psych::bassAckward() documentation.

title: Milestone history — ackwards
text: single detailed log completed milestones. CLAUDE.md carries compact one-line index (current focus); DESIGN.md §15 points . Keep file source truth milestone detail — add new milestone entry , numeric order, part milestone’s definition done. Forward-looking / deferred items ; live DESIGN.md §14 (decisions remaining) CLAUDE.md’s “scope” list. User-facing change notes live NEWS.md. M1 (done): PCA engine + algebra compute_edges() + result object + print/tidy/glance, validated psych::bassAckward(). M2 (done): ba_layout() + autoplot() (adjacent-level diagram) + suggest_k(). M3 (done): EFA engine (psych::fa(), tenBerge weights, algebra path) + materialized-scores route + algebra-vs-scores cross-check tests. M4 (done): ESEM engine (lavaan::efa(), WLSMV, tenBerge weights, rotation-aware SEs, per-level fit indices) + cor = "polychoric" engines + loadings_se level contract convergence truncation tested + estimator argument. M5 (done): Forbes extension — pairs = "", prune = "redundant"/"artefact", Tucker’s φ chains (DFS enumeration, global retain set), annotated autoplot() (skip-level arcs, pruned fill), tidy(= "nodes"), augment.ackwards() print caveat. M6 (done): Storage materialization + cfQ cleanup — scores = TRUE / keep_fits = TRUE storage, augment.ackwards(), tidy(= "scores"), cfQ hard error, cross-check tests. M7 (done): Documentation — README.Rmd, intro vignette, pkgdown site, three targeted vignettes (engines, ordinal, Forbes extension). M8 (done): Plot customization — show_r/r_digits, mono, show_level_labels/ level_label_size, node_labels, primary_only, drop_pruned/compress_levels autoplot.ackwards(); private .drop_pruned_nodes() helper layout.R. M9 (done): Visualization round 2 — show_arrows, edge_linewidth, legend autoplot.ackwards(); new ackwards-visualization.Rmd vignette; Forbes vignette slimmed toward paper; intro vignette trimmed stale comment fixed. M10 (done): Conformance + robustness — summary.ackwards() + print.summary_ackwards() (previously documented unimplemented §10 method); ESEM Heywood/improper-solution warning (theta ≤ 0, parity EFA engine); cor="spearman" + method="esem" inconsistency warning; DESIGN.md §8 reconciled list PA + MAP (EKC/EGA marked scope). M11 (done): Edge-label polish + show_r decoupling — APA .format_r() helper (strip leading zero, pad trailing zeros, suppress -.00), geom_label perpendicular offset + white halo + r_label_size arg; decouple show_r drop_pruned (default FALSE everywhere); Forbes vignette updated two-figure (labeled + unlabeled) treatment; .lintr added .Rbuildignore (R CMD check fully clean: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes). M12 (done): Best-practice suggest_k — PA-FA added alongside PA-PC (psych::fa.parallel(fa = "")); VSS-1/VSS-2 surfaced existing psych::vss() call; Comparison Data (CD) added via EFAtools::CD() gated rlang::is_installed() (skips gracefully absent); new seed arg; enriched suggest_k object (k_parallel_pc, k_parallel_fa, k_vss1, k_vss2, k_cd, cd_available, expanded criteria table); redesigned print.suggest_k() multi-criterion table; new autoplot.suggest_k() three-panel ggplot2 diagnostic (scree/PA + MAP + VSS); EFAtools added Suggests; DESIGN.md §8 §12 updated. M13 (done): Rotation honesty — removed dead kappa argument; removed rotation argument entirely (varimax valid; exposed user arg implied quartimax/equamax options); renamed “cfT” → “varimax” throughout three engine internals, result object, print output, README, docs; @section Defaults explains T′=T⁻¹ → W′RW algebra + varimax = reference papers used; DESIGN.md §4, §9, §14.1, §14.7 updated. M14 (done): Dedicated suggest_k() vignette — new vignette("ackwards-suggest-k") (“Choosing k: Many Factors?”) five criteria (pros/cons, bias direction, engine pairing), argument coverage (cor/n_iter/seed/k_max including ordinal→Pearson PA-non-reproducibility caveats), worked BFI recommendation; intro vignette Step 1 trimmed default call + pointer; _pkgdown.yml lists new article first Deep dives; README stale two-criteria description corrected five criteria; DESIGN.md §8 §15 updated. Post-review fixes: worked-example prose corrected match actual output (PA-PC=5/PA-FA=6/CD=8, CD-outlier explanation); CD table “Conservative” → “Accurate simulation; can -retain large, correlated samples”; three new tests covering autoplot.suggest_k() print.suggest_k() k_parallel_fa=NA cd_available=FALSE branches. (719 tests pass, 1 skip.) M15 (done): Naming clarity & consistency pass — k→k_max, method→engine, scores→keep_scores, align→align_signs ackwards(); $method→$engine, $cor_type→$cor result object; method→edge_method compute_edges(). S3 methods, tests, 6 vignettes, README, NEWS, CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md updated. (724 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M16 (done): Estimator-aware missing-data handling — new missing = c("pairwise","listwise", "fiml") argument ackwards(). Default "pairwise" preserves existing behaviour warns NAs present; ESEM WLSMV/ULSMV passes available.cases lavaan (full N, honest). "listwise" reduces complete cases pre-fit consistent N. "fiml" (ESEM ML/MLR ) uses Full Information ML via lavaan derives edge R FIML saturated model. Fixes ESEM ML/MLR fit-vs-edges inconsistency "listwise" "fiml" (including silent bug h1 extraction fell back pairwise). Adds .resolve_missing() helper; records meta$missing/meta$n_complete. 36 tests test-missing.R; missing-data section added ackwards-engines.Rmd; DESIGN.md §9 §14 updated. (778 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M17 (done): GitHub 0.1.0 release prep — license switched CC 4.0 → MIT (LICENSE stub + LICENSE.md updated, DESCRIPTION updated); version bumped 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0; README MIT badge comment mismatch fixed; inst/CITATION added (Goldberg 2006 + package), version field dynamic via meta[["Version"]], hand-written Goldberg prose removed README avoid duplication; README rebuilt correct two-entry citation output; NEWS.md restructured curated capability-grouped summary (development history dropped — pre-release, captured git); _pkgdown.yml 0.1.0 release URL registered; pkgdown rebuilt cleanly. Post-review: citation guard test added test-utils.R. Note: pkgdown 2.2.0 renders root .md files via package_mds() config-based exclusion — CLAUDE.md/DESIGN.md appear unlinked pages; fixable without moving files. (787 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check; URLs clean.) Tag: owner runs git tag -v0.1.0 -m "ackwards 0.1.0" && git push origin v0.1.0. M18 (done): Factor interpretation & label scaffolding — top_items() (salient per-factor item listing, |loading| >= cut, grouped cli print) label_template() (node_labels scaffold, styles: “id”/“forbes”/“blank”, prints editable c(…) literal). pure consumers existing light core; new dependencies, invariant default changes. Intro vignette visualization vignette updated; pkgdown reference index updated; DESIGN.md §10/§11/§15 updated. Post-review hardening: label_template(style = "forbes") now guards k_max > 26 (LETTERS exhaustion) loud cli_abort documents constraint; duplicate ba_layout() call forbes branch cached; sort = FALSE test asserts order tidy() rather set membership; EFA smoke tests added helpers (engine-agnosticism); stale tests/testthat/_problems/ removed. M19 (done): Dedicated interpretation/labeling vignette — documentation-. New vignette("ackwards-interpret") (“Interpreting Labeling Factors”) owns top_items() → name → label_template() → autoplot(node_labels=) workflow plus hierarchy-aware naming (parent vs child, blends, reorganizing factors via lineage/edges) sign-alignment caveat. Listed pkgdown Deep dives ackwards-suggest-k. Intro Step 5 trimmed slim top_items() example pointer; visualization vignette keeps labeling mechanic + cross-ref (naming judgment moved new vignette). DESIGN.md §15 entry (amends §10/§11). Original milestone documentation-; post-review hardening added guard test vignette’s edges idiom, top_items()/label_template()→autoplot() idiom smoke tests, switched interpret vignette cor = "polychoric" (consistency + drops ordinal warning), fixed swapped m5f3/m5f5 labels (Conscientiousness/Openness) interpret visualization vignettes. (935 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M20 (done): CRAN submission readiness + example legibility. release-readiness milestone (DESIGN.md §15 feature milestone) ahead tagging/submitting 0.1.0. Five waves: Statistical/correctness: .standardize() (na.rm-aware) replaces scale() .compute_scores() compute_edges() scores route; detect_ordinal() guarded -NA columns; stale “oblique rotations” wording compute_edges() roxygen fixed. Example conversions: \dontrun{} removed; fast examples use requireNamespace() guards; slow/stochastic suggest_k blocks use \donttest{}. Submission metadata: three DOIs added DESCRIPTION (Goldberg 2006, Waller 2007, Forbes 2023); NEWS.md restructured single 0.1.0 entry; cran-comments.md added. Example legibility: tidy.ackwards() gains sort = c("none","strength") edges; README/vignettes rewrote order(-abs(...)) → tidy(sort="strength"), identical(round(...)) → .equal(), grep("^\\.m5",...) → startsWith(names(...), ".m5"), double-rbind pattern → intermediate variable. Verify: styler + lintr clean; R CMD check ---cran → 0/0/0; README rebuilt. Owner next steps: devtools::check_win_devel(), rhub::rhub_check() actual CRAN upload. (947 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M21 (done): Onboarding & usability pass (pre-CRAN). Four parts: psych Suggests→Imports (engine substrate default PCA/EFA; never needed install prompt core use); GPArotation removed entirely (varimax routes stats::varimax; verified never loaded). check_installed("psych") guards removed; two vestigial skip_if_not_installed ("GPArotation") test lines deleted. DESIGN.md §3/§12 updated. bfi25 dataset bundled: 1 000 rows sampled psych::bfi[, 1:25] (seed 42, NAs preserved) via data-raw/bfi25.R; documented R/data.R @source (Revelle/psych/SAPA/IPIP — items public-domain IPIP). @examples + 6 vignettes + README.Rmd migrated bfi25; suggest-k worked-example prose regenerated (n=875: PA-PC=5, PA-FA=6, MAP=5, VSS-1=4, VSS-2=5, CD=6; consensus 4–6). Oracle snapshot stays full psych::bfi[, 1:25]. README “Learn ” table now lists 7 vignettes (Visualization Interpreting & labeling missing). autoplot.suggest_k() adds 4th “CD (RMSE, minimize)” panel (2×2 grid CD available; unchanged 3-panel single-column otherwise); suggest_k object gains cd_rmse field. CD vline removed MAP panel. Tests 4-panel/3-panel branches cd_rmse field. Post-review: committed stale README hero figure (bfi25 render); added test-data.R (6 assertions bfi25 shape/cols/class/NAs/range); corrected data.R @source year 2025→2026. (974 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M22 (done): Correlation-matrix input (PCA/EFA-). ackwards() suggest_k() now accept pre-computed correlation matrix detected automatically (square, symmetric, unit diagonal). New n_obs arg (required EFA+R, optional PCA+R). ESEM gated (lavaan needs raw data). cor/missing args ignored+warned R input; $cor stored NA; print shows "(user-supplied matrix)". keep_scores=TRUE/augment()/tidy(="scores") error clearly. CD gated suggest_k() info note. Edges R-matrix raw-data paths identical within floating-point tolerance (W’RW algebra). .is_cor_matrix() + .validate_cor_matrix() + .check_maybe_cov_matrix() helpers utils.R. meta$input_type field added. Non-ASCII chars replaced across R files (0/0/0 clean). Post-review: non-PD warning tested; covariance-matrix detection added (targeted error via .check_maybe_cov_matrix() functions); prune="redundant" + cor_matrix test; autoplot.suggest_k() + cor_matrix test; invalid n_obs tests suggest_k(); missing(missing) comment; NEWS folded 0.1.0. 44 tests test-cor-input.R, 41 test-utils.R. (1035 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 check.) M23 (done): Test-coverage hardening — raised covr 93.37% → 100% overall (every file 100%). Strategy: test first (# nocov genuinely unreachable defensives). New tests: suggest_k.R cor-ignored/spearman+CD/CD-dash branches; label_template.R k>26 guard; prune.R .tucker_phi -zeros / .phi_pairs adjacent / print.ackwards artefact+phi-note; compute_edges.R algebra explicit path; engine_esem.R NULL-SE skip tidy(="loadings_se"); summary.R EFA chi/dof row, empty-redundant “(none)”, phi-note, empty-lineage. # nocov markers unreachable engine defensives: PCA + EFA k=1 sign flip, EFA convergence-fail break + tenBerge fallback, ESEM lavaan version guard + convergence fail + std_sol NULL + tenBerge fallback + W NULL + warning muffler + null-fit + cov2cor unreachable branch + Pearson fallback + fitMeasures error handler + Phi shape fallback; prune.R empty-chains guard + null-nodes branch; layout.R top-zero-weight + bottom-orphaned-parent fallbacks; summary.R PCA eigenvalue miss + null-nodes branch. behavior change. Local verification (CI coverage workflow). Post-review: rewrote print.top_items empty-shown-level test deterministically hit interpret.R next (skipping bfi25 -> covered line + dropped spurious 2nd skip); # nocov’d EFA k=1 sign flip match PCA analogue; added covr-limitation note explaining ESEM warning-muffler # nocov (live code covr instrument, dead code). (1080 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) Test-suite speedup (post-M23, source/behavior change): EFAtools::CD() dominated test time (~8 s/call full 2800×25 psych::bfi vs ~0.1 s fa.parallel + vss). test-suggest_k.R rewritten run CD-bound structural/logic/print/autoplot tests small bfi25 subsets (CD ~0.8 s, cached), keeping CD/PA/MAP/VSS real — data smaller — plus one full-psych::bfi integration smoke n_obs=2800/n_vars=25 assertion. PA-cap test moved bfi25[, 1:15]/k_max=2 (genuine clamp). devtools::test() ~80 s → ~46 s; coverage held 100%. Also fixed three leaked secondary warnings suite warning-free: spearman/CD n_factors_max notice (test-suggest_k.R) NA-pairwise + ordinal notices two test-cor-input.R n_obs-ignored tests (switched continuous/complete data within-CD k_max); added deterministic default-k_max test preserve suggest_k.R:208 branch. Comfortably within CRAN’s check-time budget. (1083 tests pass, 1 skip; 100% coverage.) M24 (done): Vignette communication pass (documentation-). Stacked long-format kable comparison tables replaced wide gt tables (one row per item/edge, one column per engine/basis, explicit Δ column) ackwards-engines (loadings: |EFA|−|PCA|; edges: EFA−PCA) ackwards-ordinal (loadings: poly−pearson; edges: poly−pearson). Factor/sign alignment asserted via stopifnot() differencing; engine disagreements primary parents surface NA. ackwards-forbes: prune-nodes raw tidy() print replaced styled gt table; narrated counts stale skip-edge claim (m3f2→m5f1, now m3f2→m5f2 bfi25) converted inline `r` expressions. skip-edges/thresholds tables migrated gt visual consistency. presentation code echo = FALSE — plumbing visible readers. Audit four vignettes: changes needed (raw console prints pedagogically appropriate). gt added Suggests. Post-review (six findings): unified every Δ column magnitude convention (|x|−|y|) directional captions read correctly negatively-signed cells — fixes sign-trap polychoric-strengthened negative edge m2f2→m3f2 shown negative signed Δ (ordinal edges + loadings engines edges switched signed abs; engines loadings already abs); derived forbes redundant-level prose (flagged_ids + per-level lvl_summary) inline instead hard-coding “entire k=4 level / two factors k=2,3”; converted prune-artefact raw print styled gt table (call still echoed) inline n_artefact; added guard tests knitr::kable fallback branch, NA primary-parent-disagreement merge, magnitude-delta sign property, inline-derivation helpers. (1123 tests pass, 1 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M25 (done): Deferred-items pass — three waves. (1) suggest_k() gains criteria arg (rlang::arg_match(multiple=TRUE)); subset five criteria may requested; non-requested k_* fields → NA; shared computation (fa.parallel(fa="") / vss() called ); print() / autoplot() render requested criteria; consensus requested . (2) prune="artefact" now populates x$prune$structural — per-factor few_items / orphan / split_merge signals (Forbes Fig 2); new min_items = 3L orphan_r = 0.5 args; flag/report , never auto-prune; print()/summary() report flagged count. (3) redundancy_phi = NULL auto-resolves: PCA → φ (exact algebra); EFA/ESEM → 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006; announced via cli, Invariant 6); NA explicit opt-. Bootstrap CIs edges remain deferred. CLAUDE.md Resolved defaults + scope updated; DESIGN §9/§14 updated. Post-review hardening: added loud validation min_items (positive integer) / orphan_r ([0,1]); roxygen defaults (three-indicator rule; moderate-correlation rationale); deterministic unit tests previously-untested split_merge = TRUE path (mock loadings) Wave-3 φ-decision outcome change (.find_redundant_chains flags -φ φ>0.95) plus end--end EFA subset-property test (auto-φ flagged set ⊆ |r|-set); documented criteria= ackwards-suggest-k.Rmd, structural signals + φ auto-default ackwards-forbes.Rmd, criteria= DESIGN §8; simplified unreachable n_struct branch # nocov’d EFAtools-absent CD note (covered EFAtools missing). Coverage back 100%. (1219 tests pass, 2 skip; 0/0/0 R CMD check.) M26 (done): ESEM performance large item sets — two complementary speedups, behaviour change. (1) Cached sample statistics: ESEM engine longer recomputes lavaan’s data-derived sample statistics (thresholds, polychoric matrix, asymptotic weight matrix NACOV/WLS.V) every level — depend data, nfactors, harvested anchor level (k=1) via fit@SampleStats reused every deeper level lavaan’s slotSampleStats= argument. Verified bit-identical (0.00e+00 loading/edge diff) vs. -raw path. Dominant saving large p (polychoric+NACOV recompute O(p²)+ × k_max).
